name: MEXC Cryptocurrency Exchange API
what_it_does: Cryptocurrency exchange REST and WebSocket API — enabling traders and algorithmic trading systems to access spot and futures market data, execute trades across 1,500+ trading pairs, manage orders, stream real-time prices via WebSocket, and access MEXC's wide altcoin selection, with API-key-based authentication and compatibility with Binance API format for easier migration.
use_cases: Executing algorithmic spot trades via MEXC REST API for automated trading strategies
Streaming real-time price data via MEXC WebSocket API for trading bots and analytics
Trading new altcoin listings via MEXC API for early access to emerging tokens
Managing futures positions via MEXC Futures API for derivatives trading automation
Migrating from Binance via MEXC API due to Binance-compatible endpoint format
not_for: KYC-strict regulated environments (MEXC has limited KYC requirements — use Coinbase for compliance)
Fiat-only trading or USD bank transfers (MEXC is primarily crypto-to-crypto)
Institutional-grade custody and reporting (use Coinbase Prime or Binance Institutional)
best_when: A trader needs access to a wide altcoin selection (1,500+ pairs) with high-speed listing of new tokens, or needs a Binance-compatible API for migration with minimal code changes.
avoid_when: You need fiat on-ramp, strict regulatory compliance, or institutional-grade custody and reporting.

auth.notes: API Key + API Secret for HMAC-SHA256 request signing. X-MEXC-APIKEY header for key. signature parameter appended to query string. timestamp parameter required (within 5000ms). Binance-compatible signature format. API key permissions: Read, Spot Trade, Futures Trade, Withdrawal. No OAuth2. WebSocket auth via listenKey (obtained via REST API).

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; spot trading 0% maker / 0.1% taker (MX token discounts apply)
pricing.paid_tiers: VIP tiers based on 30-day trading volume with reduced fees
pricing.notes: Singapore-based exchange. Founded 2018. 10M+ registered users. 1,500+ trading pairs. Known for: early altcoin listings (often first to list new tokens). MX Token for fee discounts. Spot and futures markets. MEXC banned in US (unregistered exchange per CFTC/SEC). Notable: 0% maker fee for spot (promotional, may change). Binance API compatibility: v3 spot API format is intentionally Binance-compatible — existing Binance bot code works with minimal modification. No fiat withdrawal to banks (crypto only). Popular with yield hunters who want new token exposure.

performance.rate_limits: 500 requests/10 seconds for most endpoints; WebSocket: 10 messages/second

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HMAC-SHA256 signing. Binance-compatible format. 500 req/10sec. 1,500+ pairs. Banned in US. 0% maker fee. WebSocket via listenKey. Singapore company.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order creation is not idempotent — retries create duplicate orders. Use returned orderId for status checks. Cancel operations idempotent by orderId.
known_agent_gotchas: Binance-compatible API: existing Binance code works with base URL change and MEXC key
MEXC banned in US — US users cannot legally use the exchange
timestamp must be within 5000ms of server time — sync clocks carefully
listenKey for WebSocket: POST /api/v3/userDataStream, expires after 60 minutes (extend with PUT)
0% maker fee is promotional — verify current fee schedule before building strategy assumptions
Order not idempotent — check open orders via GET /api/v3/openOrders before placing

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 signing with timestamp. API key permission scopes. Singapore regulation. MEXC not registered with US CFTC/SEC — regulatory risk. Crypto-only (no fiat) reduces banking risk. No OAuth2. Timestamp prevents replay attacks (5000ms window). Cryptocurrency financial data — high value target.
